top_signals.title: US Consumer Spending Picks Up as Inflation Hits Three-Year High
top_signals.summary:
US consumer spending accelerated in May even as prices rose at the fastest pace in more than three years, suggesting Americans are powering through the fallout from the Iran war.
Rob Sockin, Chief US Economist at PGIM Credit, reacts to the PCE data and explains how the Fed might react to the numbers. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China Raises €5 Billion in Record Euro Bond Sale
top_signals.summary: China sold €5 billion ($5.7 billion) of euro-denominated bonds, its biggest ever deal in the currency and the second in seven months.
